steps.title: Understand the context
steps.content: The passage says the exhibit is "essential viewing" — strongly recommending it. The blank must be a word that would make skipping the exhibit a mistake. You need a verb meaning to ignore or pass over something worthwhile.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "complicate": "It would be a mistake to complicate the exhibit" — adding difficulty is not what the text warns against; the text is about attention, not complexity. ✗
  • "amplify": "a mistake to amplify" would mean enhancing the exhibit is wrong, which contradicts praising it as essential. ✗
  • "overlook": "a mistake to overlook" means it would be wrong to ignore or miss the exhibit — perfectly consistent with calling it "essential viewing." ✓
  • "assemble": The exhibit is already assembled and praised; cautioning against assembling it makes no sense. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
